title: alchemilla vulgaris subsp. obtusata
intro: Alchemilla vulgaris subsp. obtusata, commonly known as lady's mantle, is a perennial flowering plant native to Europe and Western Asia. It has been used in herbal medicine for centuries, valued for its anti-inflammatory and astringent properties, which help to soothe and protect the mucous membranes, skin, and digestive tract. The medicinal properties of lady's mantle have been shown to promote digestion, alleviate menstrual cramps, and reduce symptoms of menopause, while also acting as an antiseptic to prevent infection. Lady's mantle can be consumed as a tea, used as a tincture, or applied topically as a salve or ointment to harness its medicinal benefits.
